item.title: Comments Policy
item.description:
• Last Updated: 19 July 2026
  • Author / Developer: Ram Mohan Kapil
  • Application Name: Vinayak Hora
  • Platform: vinayakhora.in website

This Comments Policy explains the rules for posting comments on vinayakhora.in (the “Website”), and what happens to the information you provide when you do.

item.title: Ayana Bala: Two Ways to Measure the Same Thing (Classical Table vs Modern Trigonometry)
item.description: Ayana Bala measures a planet's strength from its northern or southern course. There are two legitimate ways to compute it — the classical kranti table and modern trigonometry. Here is how each works, why the classical texts use 24 degrees, and how Vinayak Hora and Jagannatha Hora compare on a real chart.

item.title: Natonnata Bala Made Simple: A Planet's Day–Night Strength (and Why Two Programs Disagree)
item.description: A plain-language guide to Natonnata Bala — the day/night strength in Shadbala — worked out step by step on a real example chart, and a look at why Vinayak Hora and Jagannatha Hora report different values.

item.title: Privacy Policy
item.description:
• Last Updated: 28 June 2026

  • Author / Developer: Ram Mohan Kapil

item.title: Delete My Data
item.description:
Account & Data Deletion — Vinayak Hora

App: Vinayak Hora (com.vinayakhora.vinayak_hora) Developer contact: vinayakhora@gmail.com

item.title: Terms of Use
item.description:
• Last Updated: 26 June 2026

  • Author / Developer: Ram Mohan Kapil

item.title: About Me
item.description: Contact Us: vinayakhora@gmail.com

item.title: Vinayak Hora – Advanced Astrology App
item.description: Vinayak Hora is a professional astrology app offering North & South Indian charts, D1–D60 divisional charts, Vimshottari Dasha, Shadbal, Ashtakavarga, transits, birth time correction, and Swiss Ephemeris–based calculations.

item.title: A Journey Around the World!
item.description: On Mount Kailasa, the divine abode of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati, their two sons—Kartikeya and Ganesha—grew under the watchful presence of the gods.

item.title: Why Lord Ganesha Is Worshipped First — A Vedic Perspective
item.description: A strictly Vedic explanation of why Lord Ganesha is considered first among all gods, with citations from classical Sanskrit texts.
